_about: Memgram quality eval set v1. LOCOMO-style: multi-session conversations per persona, then questions whose answers must be recalled from memory. Question types: single (stated once), multi (must combine/recall across sessions), update (a fact changed over time -> latest wins), preference, absent (never stated -> the system MUST abstain; this catches hallucination). Swap in the real LOCOMO JSON via the same shape to scale up.

sessions: I just started a new job as a backend engineer at Acme Robotics.
I mostly write Go, but the legacy services are in Python.
Please keep your answers short and to the point.
We're migrating the billing service from Python to Go this quarter.
I live in Berlin, though I'm originally from Lisbon.
I'm allergic to peanuts, so recipe suggestions should avoid them.
Quick update: I got promoted to tech lead last week.
Also I moved — I now live in Munich, not Berlin anymore.

questions.q: What programming language does the user primarily write?
What company does the user work at?
What city does the user currently live in?
What is the user's current role/title?
What food allergy should be considered?
What is the user's preferred answer style?
What kind of pet does the user have?

sessions: I'm building a mobile game in Unity with C#.
My target launch is the App Store first, Android later.
I prefer detailed explanations with code examples.
I decided to switch the backend from Firebase to Supabase for cost reasons.
I'm based in Toronto and work in the Eastern time zone.
My budget for cloud is about 200 dollars a month.

questions.q: What engine and language is the user using for the game?
Which backend did the user switch TO?
What time zone does the user work in?
What is the user's monthly cloud budget?
How does the user like answers formatted?
What is the user's spouse's name?

sessions: I'm a data scientist working mostly in Python with pandas and scikit-learn.
I'm doing research on churn prediction for a telecom client.
I've started using PyTorch for a deep learning side of the project.
I'm vegetarian, so keep that in mind for any team-lunch ideas.
I usually deploy models on Fridays.
The churn project wrapped up; now I'm on a fraud-detection project.
I switched my deploy day to Tuesdays after a few bad Friday incidents.

questions.q: What is the user's profession?
What project is the user CURRENTLY working on?
Which deep learning framework does the user use?

What is the user's dietary preference?
What car does the user drive?

sessions: I run a small e-commerce store selling handmade leather goods.
I use Shopify and want help with marketing copy.
Write in a warm, friendly tone, not corporate.
My best seller is a minimalist cardholder; the wallets sell slowly.
I'm expanding to the US market next month; currently I'm only in India.

questions.q: What platform does the user's store run on?
What is the user's best-selling product?
Which new market is the user expanding to?
What tone does the user want in writing?
How many employees does the user have?
